degraded: F0 suite RED on ONE pre-existing, FOREIGN failure: test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact flags a decision-drop belonging to iterate-2026-07-18-fr-existence-gate. Gitignored decision-drops resolve from the MAIN root, so a concurrent iterate's drop reddens this worktree. The run_id in the failure is not this run's - inherited, not introduced. CI never sees it (.gitignore:173 excludes the decision-drops dir).

SCOPE IS DELIBERATELY INCOMPLETE - the defect class is NOT closed. The brief enumerated six read sites; Stage-2 code review found a 7th and 8th and pointed at the verifier package; an exhaustive sweep found the rest. ELEVEN are fixed here, FIVE remain live on the pre-fix idiom (adopt/validate_adoption, grade/routing, phase_quality/_resolution, verifiers/adopt_compliance, run/observability). Filed as trg-360e494f with exact locations. Deferred to keep the diff reviewable, not because they are believed harmless - and note the enumeration was already wrong twice, at six and at eight.

The predecessor's stated reachability mechanism (union merge CREATES a concatenated line by joining an unterminated blob to the next side) was NOT reproducible on git 2.54 across five scenarios - git tracks the missing newline as a diff property and reconciles it. The verified mechanism is that union merge PROPAGATES an existing concatenation into main. The fix is unchanged; only the justification is corrected. Pinned in BOTH directions by the integration test so the discredited claim cannot silently return.

finalized_run_ids' corrupt policy was left UNCHANGED against the advice of both external reviewers (each rated it HIGH). Widening None to cover unrecoverable fragments contradicts a pinned test (test_arch_drift_event_scope.test_finalized_run_ids_skips_corrupt_lines) and the function's documented contract, and is a policy change rather than record recovery. Filed as an open follow-up rather than settled inside a defect repair.

surface_verification.note: The FIRST F0.5 attempt failed (exit 3) and caught a real defect in this iterate's own integration test: an in-process `from scripts.lib.collectors import ...` was ambiguous between the repo-root `scripts/` and the compliance plugin's `scripts/` - the same ADR-045 collision class this iterate is about, one namespace level up. Fixed by running the tier-B readers as their own process (the production shape, mirroring test_fold_map_e2e), verified non-vacuous, then re-run green.
